transliteration:
teṣām evānukampārtham aham ajñāna-jaṁ tamaḥ
nāśayāmy ātmabhāva-stho jñāna-dīpena bhāsvatā
literal_meaning: For them, out of compassion, I—dwelling within—destroy the darkness born of ignorance with the radiant lamp of knowledge.
contextual_meaning: When a person turns toward the divine with sincere longing, the inner intelligence (symbolized by Kṛṣṇa) responds by illuminating the unconscious shadows that keep the person stuck in repetitive fear and self-doubt.
psychological_state: Secure attachment to an inner source of wisdom; reduction of cognitive distortions; emergence of self-efficacy.
trigger_scenario: A college student who has always felt 'not smart enough' sits down to write her thesis, suddenly recalls a mentor’s encouragement, and experiences a clear idea that dissolves her writer’s block.
anchor_text: Turn to the divine and inner light dissolves the darkness of ignorance.
